purpose: Single-export `validateDependencyGraph(agents)` that performs four structural checks on a Synapse swarm's agent dependency graph and returns `{valid, errors, warnings}`. Errors (block dispatch): (A) self-references — an agent listing its own id in `depends_on`; (B) dangling references — `depends_on` entries that aren't in the agents array; (C) circular dependencies — detected via Kahn's algorithm (BFS topological sort): builds in-degree map and adjacency list, enqueues all in-degree-0 nodes, drains the queue while decrementing neighbor in-degrees; if `sorted.length < agents.length` after the BFS, the remaining nodes are in a cycle and reported by id. Warnings (advisory): (D) orphan tasks — non-Wave-1 tasks with no dependencies AND nothing depending on them (Wave 1 is exempt because Wave 1 tasks are root tasks by design). Wave value is normalized via `parseInt` if string. Used by WatcherService when initialization.json is written to gate dispatch, and by master agent pre-flight planning checks.

exports.name: validateDependencyGraph
exports.signature: (agents: Array<{id, depends_on?, wave?}>) => {valid: boolean, errors: Array<{type,message}>, warnings: Array<{type,message}>}

gotchas: Kahn's algorithm REQUIRES an acyclic graph for full topo sort — the cycle-detection is implicit (`sorted.length < agents.length`), and the reported cycle nodes are ALL non-sorted nodes, not just the minimal cycle. Diagnostics may include innocent downstream nodes blocked by the cycle.

Dangling references are reported as ERRORS but the cycle check still runs and only adds edges where `idSet.has(dep)` — so a graph with both dangling refs and cycles will report both, but the cycle node list excludes the dangling-ref tasks.
Self-references generate BOTH a self_ref error AND a cycle error (the self-loop creates an in-degree of 1 that's never decremented) — duplicate signal, may confuse log readers.

Wave 1 exemption uses `wave === 1` after `parseInt` — a wave value of `'1.0'` parses to 1 and is exempt, but `'01'` parses to 1 too; non-numeric strings parseInt to NaN and the agent is NOT exempt (will be flagged orphan if it has no deps).
Orphan check is O(N*M) where M is avg deps — fine for typical swarms (<100 tasks) but could be slow on very large graphs.
`agents` parameter is assumed to be an array of objects with at least `id` — the function will throw `TypeError` if any agent is null or lacks id. No defensive check at the entry.
patterns: Kahn's algorithm for cycle detection: build in-degree + adjacency, BFS from in-degree-0 nodes, cycle iff sorted.length < total.
Four-phase validation: self_ref -> dangling_ref -> cycle -> orphan. Each phase appends to errors/warnings independently — all checks always run, no short-circuit.
Errors block, warnings inform: only errors flip `valid: false`.
Wave 1 exemption convention — root tasks have no upstream dependencies and may have no downstream dependents (e.g. terminal write tasks).
Type discrimination via `type` field on each error/warning ('self_ref', 'dangling_ref', 'cycle', 'orphan') — UI uses this to color/group.
conventions: Errors and warnings carry both `type` (machine-readable) and `message` (human-readable).
Wave is a 1-indexed integer (or numeric string).
